# Test fixtures: trimmed DISA XCCDF sysctl-key extracts

Each `<product>_sysctld_controls.xml` is a trimmed slice of the real, official,
publicly-releasable DISA STIG XCCDF benchmark for that product, containing only
the `<Group>`/`<Rule>` elements the sysctld STIG-baseline selector cares about,
plus 2 decoy Groups per file so the selector-exclusion tests have real,
non-synthetic negative fixtures. `check-content`/`fixtext`/`title` text is
copied VERBATIM (byte-for-byte raw substring extraction, not re-serialized XML)
from the source benchmark; the real `<Benchmark>` root's full `xmlns:*`
namespace declarations are carried into the fixture root so namespace-prefixed
sub-elements (`<xhtml:...>`, `<cpe:...>`, etc.) inside a copied `<Group>`
resolve without an "unbound prefix" parse error. No non-ASCII characters were
found in any of the three trimmed files (grep-confirmed against the repo's
ASCII-only convention) - unlike the auditd fixture precedent, no
transliteration was needed here.

DEV-ONLY: these fixtures exist to make `tools/stig-update`'s tests run OFFLINE,
without depending on the DISA CDN being reachable during a PR. They are not
shipped in the `rulesteward` release binary or its distribution artifacts.

## Provenance

| Product | Source benchmark | Real (selected) Groups | Decoy Groups |
|---|---|---:|---:|
| rhel8  | RHEL 8 STIG V2R8 (`U_RHEL_8_V2R8_STIG.zip`)   | 31 | 2 |
| rhel9  | RHEL 9 STIG V2R9 (`U_RHEL_9_V2R9_STIG.zip`)   | 33 | 2 |
| rhel10 | RHEL 10 STIG V1R2 (`U_RHEL_10_V1R2_STIG.zip`) | 32 | 2 |

These are the SAME three DISA zip filenames `tools/sshd-stig-update/stig-refs.toml`
and `tools/auditd-stig-update/stig-refs.toml` already pin - all three sysctld,
sshd, and auditd baselines are drawn from the same three benchmark documents;
see `../../stig-refs.toml` in this tool for the pinned CDN URL.

## Selector (which Groups are "real"/selected)

A `<Group>`/`<Rule>` pair is a settable-sysctl-key requirement IFF its
`check-content` invokes `sysctl <dotted.key>` (with or without a leading
`sudo`) and ALSO echoes a `<dotted.key>[ ]=[ ]<value>` line for that SAME key
(tolerating both `key = value` and `key=value` spacing) - grounded in
`/mnt/side-projects/9h-v0_8-wave4/lane-b-grounding.md` section 2, this
test-author's own inventory of every `sysctl`/`/proc/sys`-mentioning Group
across all three pinned benchmarks (96 real hits + 2 near-miss FIPS Groups,
every one read in full, not sampled).

## The decoys (2 per file, real DISA Groups NOT in the real/selected set)

1. **rhel9/rhel10**: the real FIPS-mode check (V-258230 / V-281009,
   `crypto.fips_enabled`) - a genuine near-miss that mentions `/proc/sys`
   (`cat /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled`) but does NOT use the `sysctl <key>`
   command+echo idiom, proving the selector's exclusion actually filters
   something non-trivial (mirrors the `auditd-stig-update` fixture precedent's
   decoy rationale). **rhel8 substitutes a second generic decoy here**
   (V-230223, the systemwide crypto-policy check) because rhel8's own STIG has
   no `/proc/sys`-based FIPS check at all (it checks via
   `update-crypto-policies --show` instead) - verified: every rhel8 Group that
   mentions `sysctl` in its `check-content` is already in the real/selected
   set (grounding doc section 4b).
2. **All three products**: a short, wholly unrelated "there must be no
   `.shosts` files" check (V-230284 rhel8 / V-257956 rhel9 / V-281272 rhel10) -
   a trivial negative control with zero sysctl/proc-sys mention at all.

## Regenerating

These files were generated by one-off scripts against the three pinned zips
fetched fresh from `https://dl.dod.cyber.mil/wp-content/uploads/stigs/zip/`
(not committed; see `/mnt/side-projects/9h-v0_8-wave4/lane-b-grounding.md` for
the full inventory + per-key diff against the shipped `baseline.rs` tables). If
the pinned DISA revision bumps, regenerate by re-running the same selector over
the new benchmark and reviewing the diff before committing - do not hand-edit
these files.
